regulatory_context.value: Article 4 requires AI providers and deployers to ensure sufficient AI literacy; the obligation applies from 2 February 2025 and supervision/enforcement starts from 3 August 2026.

training_support_route.value: Luxembourg company-training support exists at scheme level: the State contributes to employer training plans, with 15% base aid and 35% salary-cost weighting for certain eligible participants.

value: House of Training - Prompt Engineering (finance-sector e-learning)
House of Training - Entrepreneur augmente grace a ChatGPT
House of Training - AI risk / data protection / AI course
No empty-market or knowledge-scarcity claim survives; public local courses and free public prompting guidance already exist.
The rerun proves only scheme-level Luxembourg training support, not workshop-level subsidy eligibility.
The proposed prompt-library deliverable is still an internal promise, not an evidenced fact.
Customization as the main differentiator remains a thesis, not a verified truth-layer field.
The exact 1-2 day format and output bundle remain unverified product design choices.

target_audience_summary.value: Luxembourg employers already using AI tools and wanting more structured prompt practice within a governance-aware setting.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Useful synthesis, but still partly inferential for the exact workshop.

product_specific_subsidy_eligibility.omission_reason: Public support routes exist, but this exact workshop's eligibility was not evidenced.

prompt_library_deliverable.omission_reason: The 30-50 tested prompts deliverable is not a public fact.

customization_differentiator.omission_reason: The rerun did not find public evidence proving customization is the winning wedge.

exact_workshop_format.omission_reason: The 1-2 day format and prompt-library outcome are internal design choices, not verified facts.
